name: Type-Route
description: Type-safe routing library focused on inference, framework-agnostic core with React bindings

description.value: The flexible, type safe routing library
description.comment: Official tagline; TypeScript-first router with pure type inference, built on the history library that also powers React Router.

category.comment: Drop-in router you add to an existing React (or non-React) app; framework-agnostic core with optional React bindings.

active-maintenance.comment: Latest stable release 1.1.0 was 2023-12-09 and last repo push was 2024-03-14 — no commits or releases within the last ~6 months as of 2026-07-14.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Last-week downloads of the `type-route` package, checked 2026-07-14.

routing-paradigm.comment: Routes are declared as a config object via createRouter/defineRoute and consumed through the useRoute() hook; no component-based <Route> elements or file-based routing.

nested-layout-routes.comment: Only offers grouping of flat routes via .extend() and createGroup() — there is no true hierarchical nesting with inherited parent layouts.

dynamic-params.comment: Path params via param.path.string / .number etc., e.g. /user/${userId}.

optional-catch-all-params.comment: Supports optional path params (param.path.optional) and trailing/catch-all wildcard params (param.path.trailing).

search-params-api.comment: First-class typed query-string API via param.query.* builders (string/number/boolean, optional, arrays, custom serializers).

route-guards-middleware.comment: Offers a global session.block() navigation blocker but no per-route guards, middleware, or beforeLoad-style lifecycle hooks.

written-in-typescript.comment: Authored in TypeScript; the library's core purpose is TS type safety.

type-safe-paths-links.comment: Link generation and navigation go through the typed routes object, so calls are checked against defined routes.

type-safe-params.comment: Path params are inferred with correct types at usage sites (e.g. route.params.userId typed as string).

type-safe-search-params.comment: Query params are typed and coerced via param.query.* builders (number/boolean/custom serializers).

codegen-required.comment: Type safety comes from pure TypeScript inference — no code generation or build step required.

type-safety-rating.comment: Type-safe paths, params, and search params via pure inference (no codegen). Not a 5 because it has no data loaders to type end-to-end.

route-data-loaders.comment: No route-level loader concept; data fetching is left entirely to the app.

actions-mutations.comment: No built-in action/mutation or form-handling primitive.

prefetching.comment: No route code/data prefetching on hover or intent.

pending-transition-ui.comment: No built-in pending state or React transition integration during navigation.

scroll-restoration.comment: Scrolls to top on new route pushes by default; can opt out with scrollToTop: false for custom logic.

per-route-code-splitting.comment: No built-in per-route splitting; the docs point you to plain React.lazy + Suspense on your page components.

per-route-error-handling.comment: No route-scoped error elements/boundaries; error handling is left to your own React error boundaries.

ssr-support.comment: Documented SSR support via router.session.reset with memory history, then ReactDOM.hydrate — DIY wiring (example uses Fastify), not turnkey.

streaming-ssr.comment: SSR guide only covers renderToString + hydrate; no streaming/suspenseful SSR documented.

react-server-components.comment: No RSC support; it is a client-side router with optional DIY SSR.

static-site-generation.comment: No built-in pre-rendering/SSG feature; the library is a runtime client router.

client-only-usable.comment: Primary use case is a pure client-side SPA router with browser history.

framework-bound.comment: Drop-in library with no build/framework toolchain of its own; works with React, other UI libraries, or none.

bundle-size.comment: Minified + gzipped size of type-route@1.1.0 per Bundlephobia (9,762 bytes; ~30 KB minified).

zero-runtime-dependencies.comment: Has one runtime dependency — the history library that also powers React Router. React is a peer dependency.

tree-shakeable.comment: Package reports hasSideEffects: false, enabling dead-code elimination.

documentation-quality.comment: Dedicated docs site with getting-started, topic guides (SSR, code splitting, wildcards, scroll restoration), and a full API reference with examples. Not a 5 (no interactive playground or migration recipes).

data-fetching-integration.comment: Routing-only library with no documented first-class data-fetching integration; you bring your own data layer.

predecessor-lineage.comment: No predecessor router; it is an independent project (built atop the shared history library, not a successor to a specific router).
